requirement.title: Sprint Model — Pure Function, Overwrite DAG Each Sprint
requirement.description: Planner is a pure function (spec + delta) → (dag.yaml + epics/). It MUST build dag.yaml and epics/ from scratch each time and MUST overwrite any existing DAG artifacts without archiving. The scope is determined by the spec's <delta> element whose version equals the current spec_version. For v1 (no <delta> anywhere in <overview>), planner plans all requirements currently in detail files. DAG is a disposable derived view — historical traceability lives in the spec's accumulated <delta> elements and in design doc iteration folders, not in archived DAGs.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When no <delta> is present in spec.xml (v1), planner MUST produce a dag.yaml with epics covering all requirements in the spec. All epics start in "pending" status. REQ-F017

When one or more <delta> elements are present, planner MUST select the <delta> whose version attribute equals the current spec_version. Every child of that <delta> MUST generate an epic: <added> → implement the new requirement; <modified> → update the existing requirement's implementation; <removed> → teardown epic that references the removed ref (implementer LLM greps and removes tied code); <renamed> → mechanical code-side refactor epic that updates references from ref_old to ref_new. Prior <delta> elements (version != current spec_version) are historical record; planner MUST NOT use them for scope. REQ-F017

If a previous dag.yaml exists, planner MUST overwrite it. Planner MUST NOT write any archive file (e.g., dag.yaml.archive.YYYY-MM-DD) or move the previous dag.yaml to a backup location. Previous epic statuses MUST NOT carry over. Historical DAG state, if needed by a reader, lives in git history of dag.yaml — arcforge does not treat git as part of its contract but does not prevent inspection. REQ-F017
For each epic in dag.yaml, planner MUST produce an epics/<epic-id>/epic.md with at minimum: title, description, and feature breakdown. REQ-F017

A <delta> containing only <removed> children (pure teardown sprint) MUST be accepted as a legal sprint scope. Planner MUST NOT block or warn based on the composition of a <delta> element — only per-child correctness is enforced. REQ-F017

Planner MUST NOT implement any completion gate, archive mechanism, or state-preservation logic. These responsibilities belong upstream (refiner, per fr-rf-012). Planner is a pure function with no state beyond its output paths. REQ-F017

requirement.title: 1:1 Mapping for Traceability
requirement.description: Planner MUST maintain strict mapping between spec structure and DAG structure for full traceability.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each <detail> in spec.xml MUST map to at least one epic. A large detail MAY be split into multiple epics. REQ-F017
Each <requirement> MUST map to exactly one feature. The feature's source_requirement field MUST reference the spec requirement ID. REQ-F017
Dependencies between requirements (<dependency ref>) MUST be translated to depends_on relationships between features/epics. REQ-F017

requirement.title: Self-Contained Project Unit
requirement.description: DAG and epics MUST live inside the spec folder. Each specs/<spec-id>/ is a self-contained project unit with all artifacts needed for execution.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: dag.yaml MUST be written to specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml, not to the project root or any other location. REQ-F022
Epic folders MUST be at specs/<spec-id>/epics/<epic-id>/. REQ-F022

requirement.title: Done Signal
requirement.description: A planning round is considered "done" when all epics in the DAG are in "completed" status. This is the signal that the current spec has been fully implemented — the next sprint's refiner is unblocked and the next planner run will overwrite this DAG.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a dag.yaml with 5 epics and all 5 in "completed" status, the planning round MUST be considered done. REQ-F023
Given a dag.yaml with 5 epics and 1 still "pending", the planning round MUST NOT be considered done. REQ-F023

requirement.title: Input Validation — Spec Format
requirement.description: Before any decomposition, planner MUST validate its input: the spec.xml identity header per fr-cc-if-002. All validation runs before any output is produced.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Planner MUST verify specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists and its identity header conforms to fr-cc-if-002. Missing spec or invalid header is ERROR with remediation: "Run refiner to produce a spec first." REQ-F016
Planner MUST verify source/design_path in the spec header points to an existing file. Broken pointer is WARNING (not blocking — the spec itself is valid, but provenance tracing is broken). REQ-F016

requirement.title: Output Validation — DAG Integrity
requirement.description: Planner MUST follow the two-pass write pattern (fr-cc-val-001): build the complete dag.yaml and epics/ in memory, validate structural integrity, then write to disk only if validation passes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every epic in the built dag.yaml MUST have: id (string), status ("pending"), source_requirement (string referencing a spec requirement). Missing fields are ERROR. REQ-F017
No circular dependencies allowed. If the dependency graph contains a cycle, planner MUST report the cycle and block. REQ-F017
All depends_on references MUST point to existing epic/feature IDs within the same DAG. Broken references are ERROR. REQ-F017
If output validation finds ERRORs, planner MUST report all findings and MUST NOT write dag.yaml or epics/ to disk. REQ-F017
